text.title: AWS Service Selection (Compute, Database, Architecture Decisions) Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Lambda chosen for 30-min batch; team builds Step Functions chain; complexity + cost explode.
antipattern.root-cause: Author picked Lambda because 'serverless = good' without checking duration.
antipattern.fix: Switch to Fargate or AWS Batch. Lambda only for <15min handlers.

antipattern.symptom: DDB chosen for invoice system; 50% of queries are scans because access patterns weren't enumerated.
antipattern.root-cause: DDB picked for 'cool factor'; SQL needs ignored.
antipattern.fix: Switch to Aurora Serverless v2. Or stay with DDB and redesign access patterns + add GSIs.

antipattern.symptom: EKS chosen for 3-service workload; team spends 30% capacity managing node groups.
antipattern.root-cause: EKS picked for 'flexibility' without measuring real need.
antipattern.fix: Switch to ECS Fargate. EKS only justified when you need custom DaemonSets / kernel access / specific CNI.

antipattern.symptom: x86 Lambda costs 20% more; team complains about cost without recognising the easy fix.
antipattern.root-cause: Default architecture never changed; nobody asked.
antipattern.fix: Switch to arm64. Rebuild Python wheels for arm64 (most are pre-built).
